cards.name: HC5
cards.loading_capacity: Up to 768أ—432 pixels (may vary in different applications)
cards.overview: The Colorlight HC5 is a 5G high-end receiving card designed for fine-pitch LED displays in fixed installations and rental applications. It features high-density connectors with a 5 Gbps transmission rate. Each card supports up to 768أ—432 pixels and 32 groups of parallel data or 128 groups of serial data. With compact dimensions of 80.0أ—45.0أ—16.5 mm, it readily supports higher frame rates and color depth than conventional Gigabit Ethernet systems. It also increases the load capacity per cable, enabling more cabinets to be cascaded with reduced cabling, improving cabinet design flexibility and overall system stability. Beyond standard receiving card functions, the HC5 provides practical and powerful features tailored for high-end displays. It supports larger control areas and higher-precision calibration, delivering superior image quality and performance while adding value to customer products. The HC5 is widely used in defense and security, broadcasting, smart surveillance, education and research, stage rental, and other fields.

cards.name: i5A
cards.loading_capacity: Full-color: 256أ—256 pixels, the column can be extended to 1024 pixels for special applications
cards.overview: The i5A receiving card adopts a new generation of image processing core, which greatly improves the display effect. It supports higher level of color input and gray level output, which create more delicate images. Great improvement has been made on low gray scale performance. Higher refresh rates and more stable display images can be achieved based on high-quality display effect. In addition to inheriting all the advantages and features the 5A series receiving cards boast, i5A adopts a new generation of system architecture, and possesses more intelligent functions and more stable systems. The i5A receiving card supports a range of more intelligent and high-end functions like arbitrary switching between programs, and external connection to the LCD display module, leading the LED control system to a brand-new level.

general: More perfect display effect by adopting a new processing core
High refresh rate, high gray scale and high brightness can be achieved by conventional chips
Better performance under low grayscale status
Better detail processing: partial dark at row, reddish at low gray and shadow problems can be solved
Support the effective display at first grayscale value
Support 8-bit pixel level calibration in the brightness and the chromaticity
Support up to 1/64 scan
Support any pumping point and arbitrary offset of data groups and realize various freeform display, spherical display, creative display, etc.
Support 16 groups of RGBRâ€™ outputs and 20 groups of RGB outputs, and signal outputs can be extended to 32 groups
Large loading capacity
Wide operating voltage range

Each card supports a maximum load capacity of 640أ—360 pixels, with up to 32 groups of parallel data or 64 groups of serial data (expendable to 128 groups). Thanks to its compact size of 67.6mmأ—35.5mmأ—4.2mm, the i8 is well-suited for cabinets with limited internal space, as well as fine-pitch displays. Featuring a DDR2 SODIMM interface, it can be effortlessly integrated into HUB boards or LED panels, enabling a more flexible and streamlined structural design of display modules. The i8 encompasses all the functionalities of industry-standard receiving cards, making it an ideal choice for high-end rental and fine-pitch displays. Furthermore, it offers a collection of practical and powerful features tailored for high-end applications, adding value to customer products and enhancing their overall worth.
